data.title: Crude inventory rose by 0.5M barrels for week ended April 11 – EIA
data.description:
Commercial crude stocks for the week ended April 11: 442.9M barrels.

Crude inventory change: 0.5M barrels vs. 2.6M barrels for the week ended April 4.

Consensus estimate 0.400M.

Gasoline inventory change: -2.0M barrels vs. -1.6M barrels for the week ended April 4. 

Consensus estimate -1.600M.

Distillates inventory change: -1.9M barrels vs. -3.5M barrels for the week ended April 4. 

Consensus estimate -1.200M.

SPR:  0.3M vs 0.3M for the week ended April 4.

 

 

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:DBO), (USL).

data.title: Movado highlights cost-cutting and strategic brand initiatives amid challenging environment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Movado Group, Inc. (MOV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Efraim Grinberg disclosed irregularities in the Dubai sales office, leading to the termination of its leader and restatement of financials for prior years. He emphasized the company’s commitment to ethics, transparency, and enhancing control measures.
  • Sales for the year were $653.4 million, reflecting a slight decline of 1.7% compared to the previous year. Adjusted operating income was $27.1 million, affected by increased marketing investments.
  • Q4 performance improved, with sales up by 3.3% to $181.5 million and adjusted operating profit rising to $13.5 million. Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $0.51.
  • The company ended the fiscal year with $208.5 million in cash and no debt. A quarterly dividend of $0.35 was declared.
  • COO and CFO Sallie DeMarsilis reported a gross profit margin of 54.2% for Q4, an improvement driven by favorable channel and product mix.

Outlook

  • The company refrained from providing fiscal 2026 guidance due to global economic uncertainty and tariff impacts. Management highlighted plans to protect gross margins through partnerships, selective price increases, and cost mitigation strategies.
  • Marketing expenditures are set to decrease by $15 million to $20 million in the upcoming year, aligning with a focus on digital marketing and conversion.
  • Movado anticipates annualized savings of $10 million from restructuring efforts, alongside reductions in marketing expenses.

Financial Results

  • Q4 sales were $181.5 million, a 3.3% increase compared to the same period last year. Adjusted operating income rose to $13.5 million, up from $10.8 million in Q4 2024.
  • Gross profit margin improved to 54.2%, benefiting from product mix and operational leverage.
  • Fiscal year-end cash reserves were $208.5 million, with no debt. Inventory levels increased slightly to $156.7 million.
  • For the full year, adjusted EPS was $1.12, and net income reached $25.4 million.
  • The company repurchased 120,000 shares during fiscal 2025, with $50 million remaining under the current buyback program.

Q&A

  • Hamed Khorsand, BWS Financial, questioned the marketing strategy changes and sales outlook. CEO Grinberg explained the shift toward digital venues for more flexible and efficient marketing efforts. He also highlighted the focus on execution and controllable expenses amid uncertain trade and tariff conditions.
  • Khorsand inquired about cost savings initiatives. Grinberg emphasized the company’s intention to generate free cash flow and highlighted variable expenses and inventory management as key areas of focus.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of tariffs and global economic uncertainty on sales and profitability. Their tone was cautious, reflecting skepticism about near-term growth projections.
  • Management maintained a measured and optimistic tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing cost controls and strategic initiatives. However, the tone shifted to more defensive responses during Q&A, reflecting the complexity of the current operating environment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed heightened caution, with a stronger focus on mitigating risks and managing expenses.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Compared to Q3 2025, Q4 showed a positive trend in sales growth and gross profit margin improvement. Adjusted operating income also increased quarter-over-quarter.
  • Strategic focus shifted more prominently toward cost reductions and digital marketing in Q4, reflecting a response to the challenging retail environment and tariff pressures.
  • Management’s tone remained consistent, though the emphasis on ethical practices and transparency was heightened following the Dubai office investigation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff uncertainties remain a significant concern, with management outlining plans to mitigate cost impacts through strategic partnerships and selective price adjustments.
  • The company faces ongoing challenges in the retail environment, particularly in the US and Europe, with tighter inventory management by retailers impacting sales.
  • The Dubai sales office investigation highlighted risks related to operational oversight and governance, though management has taken corrective actions.

Final Takeaway

Movado Group demonstrated resilience in Q4 2025 by achieving sales growth and improved profitability despite challenges in the retail sector and tariff uncertainties. Management's focus on cost reductions, digital marketing, and ethical practices positions the company for strengthened operations. However, the lack of fiscal 2026 guidance reflects caution amid global economic uncertainties and trade dynamics.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Vivos Therapeutics to acquire operating assets of The Sleep Center of Nevada
data.description:
• Vivos Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VVOS) said it will acquire the operating assets of The Sleep Center of Nevada (SCN)
  • The firm will pay up to $9 million for the SCN operating assets, including $6 million in cash and $1.5 million in Vivos common stock at closing.
  • An additional $1.5 million in Vivos common stock may be earned in the future if SCN achieves an agreed financial milestone.
  • The company expects to get the funding for the upfront cash payment primarily with a senior debt facility and also potential equity financing.
  • The deal is expected to close in the later part of this quarter or in the third quarter, and the proposed transaction does not require Vivos shareholder approval.
  • SCN is the largest operator of medical sleep centers in Nevada.

data.title: WTO sees 2025 contraction in global goods trade, driven by U.S., as tariffs weigh
data.description:
Uncertainty over tariffs prompted the World Trade Organization to revise down its outlook for global goods trade, forecasting a contraction this year and possibly a deeper one if conditions deteriorate further, according to a Wednesday release.

It now sees world merchandise trade volume declining 0.2% in 2025 premised on the tariff situation as of April 14, per the WTO Secretariat's latest Global Trade Outlook and Statistics report. That's almost three percentage points below what would have been anticipated under a low-tariff baseline case.

Global goods trade could experience a more severe 1.5% drop this year "if the situation deteriorates" on the tariffs front. That would come in stark contrast to 2024's 2.9% rise.

Before President Donald Trump returned to the White House, the WTO Secretariat expected a continued expansion in global trade both this year and next. But now, the introduction of numerous new tariffs since January have forced the economists to substantially downgrade their forecast for goods trade, with a smaller reduction in their forecast for services trade.

"I am deeply concerned by the uncertainty surrounding trade policy, including the US-China stand-off," WTO Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala said in a statement. "The recent de-escalation of tariff tensions has temporarily relieved some of the pressure on global trade. However, the enduring uncertainty threatens to act as a brake on global growth, with severe negative consequences for the world, the most vulnerable economies in particular."

If the current suspended tit-for-tat tariffs reactivate, that would bring down global goods trade volume growth by 0.6 percentage point in 2025, and further uncertainty from there could reduce it by another 0.8 percentage point.

The U.S., in particular, is expected to see exports tumble 12.6% and imports slide 9.6% this year, under current tariff conditions. That, in turn, would shave off 1.7 percentage points from 2025 global goods growth, the WTO said. 

The impact of recent tariff measures is expected to diverge significantly in Asia, which is projected to record a modest 1.6% advance in both exports and imports. Also, Europe's goods exports and imports are expected to grow 1.9% and 1.0%, respectively, in 2025. With trade redirected, China's goods exports are expected to rise 4%-9% across all regions outside North America.

 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: KKR is said to weigh options for Atlantic Aviation, including $10B sale
data.description:
KKR & Co. (NYSE:KKR) is considering a range of strategic options for Atlantic Aviation, a U.S.-based provider of services for private aircraft, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing sources familiar with the matter. Among the possibilities being reviewed is a sale that could value the business at approximately $10 billion.

The private-equity firm is collaborating with financial advisers to assess interest from prospective acquirers, the sources said. In addition to a full or partial sale, an initial public offering is also under consideration.

Atlantic Aviation runs a network of more than 100 fixed-base operations across the United States. It could attract interest from a variety of buyers, including airport operators, aircraft lessors and large infrastructure investment funds.

The discussions are still in early stages, and KKR may ultimately opt to retain ownership of the company, Bloomberg News reported, citing its sources who asked not to be named. KKR declined to provide a comment, and Atlantic Aviation didn’t immediately respond to inquiries to the news outlet.

This potential transaction is being evaluated amid a challenging environment for dealmaking, as global financial markets remain volatile. Despite this, investor interest in stable, infrastructure-like assets remains strong. Earlier this month, Brookfield Infrastructure Partners led a consortium in acquiring Colonial Enterprises in a roughly $9 billion deal.

Demand for aviation services

Investor appetite for fixed-base operations or related assets has been evident in past transactions. Blackstone (BX) and Global Infrastructure Partners in 2021 jointly acquired Signature Aviation, the world’s biggest private jet base operator, for $4.7 billion.

Atlantic Aviation’s services include fueling, maintenance, deicing and passenger concierge offerings, as well as leasing hangar and office space, according to its website. KKR originally purchased the company from Macquarie Infrastructure in 2021 in a deal valued at $4.5 billion.

Despite ongoing trade tensions that have dampened broader market activity, KKR has remained active in mergers and acquisitions. So far this month, it has closed or pursued deals totaling more than $8 billion. Notably, it secured OSTTRA, a post-trade services provider, in a competitive bid valued at more than $3 billion, and recently confirmed interest in acquiring German information-technology company Datagroup.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: NVDA, TRV, ASML, UAL, and more
data.description:
Stock futures slipped on Wednesday as the Trump administration intensified scrutiny of critical mineral imports, adding to existing probes into pharmaceuticals and semiconductors and fueling renewed trade tension concerns. Meanwhile, March retail sales data showed resilience, with core retail sales beating expectations and the headline figure coming in line with consensus.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) +6% – Shares rose after the carrier posted better-than-expected Q1 earnings of $0.91 per share, swinging from a loss of $0.15 a year ago and topping estimates by $0.17. Growth in its basic economy segment helped drive the beat. Capacity rose 4.9% Y/Y, while TRASM increased 0.5% and CASM dropped 3.4%. Excluding fuel, CASM rose 0.3%. International revenue climbed 5.2%, offsetting a 3.9% drop in domestic revenue. However, management warned that a recession could significantly dent profitability. For Q2, United forecasts EPS of $3.25 to $4.25, bracketing the $3.97 consensus. FY2025 guidance ranges from $11.50–$13.50 per share in a stable economy to $7.00–$9.00 in a recession, vs. the consensus of $10.36 per share.
  • The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) +4% – Shares climbed after the insurer raised its quarterly cash dividend by 4.8% to $1.10 per share and posted Q1 earnings supported by strong underlying underwriting income despite a surge in catastrophe losses, primarily from January's California wildfires. The combined ratio rose to 102.5% from 83.2% in Q4 and 93.9% in the year-ago quarter. Meanwhile, the underlying combined ratio improved to 84.8%, up from 84.0% in the prior quarter and down from 87.7% a year earlier.

Biggest stock losers

  • Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) -6% – Shares declined after the company said it received notice from the U.S. government requiring a special license to export its H20 GPUs to China indefinitely, raising fresh concerns over trade restrictions. The company expects to take a $5.5B charge in its upcoming quarterly results due to the development. The H20 chip, designed to comply with earlier export rules, had seen over $16B in orders from Chinese firms like Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance in early 2025 ahead of the anticipated ban.
  • ASML Holding (NASDAQ:ASML) -5% – Shares dropped after the Dutch semiconductor equipment maker reported mixed Q1 results and missed order expectations and flagged potential demand headwinds from new U.S. trade restrictions. Net bookings totaled €3.94B ($4.47B) in Q1 2025, falling short of analysts’ forecast of €4.89B. CEO Christophe Fouquet noted that while the long-term demand outlook remains strong, particularly with AI as a key driver, “uncertainty with some of our customers” could push revenue toward the lower end of ASML’s 2025 forecast range of €30B–€35B.
  • J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) -6% – The stock fell as investors looked past upbeat Q1 results and focused on declining revenue and margins. Operating revenue dipped 1% Y/Y to $2.92B, dragged down by a 5% reduction in average truck count in Dedicated Contract Services, 15% fewer stops in Final Mile Services, and a 13% drop in load volumes in Integrated Capacity Solutions. Operating income slid 8% to $178.7M, weighed by lower DCS revenue, weaker yields in JBI, rising insurance and medical costs, and higher equipment and maintenance expenses.
  • Interactive Brokers Group (NASDAQ:IBKR) -9% – Shares slumped after the company delivered mixed Q1 results, with earnings falling short of expectations despite a 28% dividend hike and the announcement of a 4-for-1 stock split. The profit miss appeared to overshadow the shareholder-friendly moves, weighing on investor sentiment. The stock split will take effect after market close on June 17, with trading on a split-adjusted basis beginning June 18.
  • Kingsoft Cloud (NASDAQ:KC) -16% – Shares tumbled after the Chinese cloud services provider announced a public offering of 18.5M American depositary shares (ADS), each representing 15 ordinary shares, totaling 277.5M ordinary shares. Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase additional ADSs. In a concurrent private placement, major shareholder Kingsoft Corporation (OTCPK:KSFTF) agreed to buy additional shares at the public offering price. The company plans to use the net proceeds to upgrade and expand its infrastructure, invest in technology and product development, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow fall as Nvidia's China licensing requirement fuels trade war concerns
data.description:
Major averages were lower on Wednesday, as worries about a U.S.-China trade war rose on news that Nvidia will need a special license to ship graphic processing units to China.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.32%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was lower by 5 basis points to 3.80%.

The core retail sales beat consensus in March, while the headline number came in line

Wall Street on Tuesday ended slightly lower in a choppy trading session, as a boost from positive bank earnings petered out amid more caution over developments surrounding tariffs.

"After a strong start to the week, the market mood turned more negative again yesterday, as tensions between the US and China showed signs of further escalation," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"So after a brief period of greater stability in markets, that reminded investors about the ongoing risks of escalation, raising fears that the trade war could still get worse from here," Allen added.

Nvidia (NVDA) fell 6.5% as it received a notice from the U.S. government that it needs a special license to ship its H20 graphic processing units or related hardware to China, and such a license will be required indefinitely.

"Some politicians may believe trade is more important than it is. Trade does matter economically, but a 'made in China' product in a store does not mean all the price paid goes to China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

President Donald Trump has also directed an investigation to determine if U.S. critical minerals imports "threaten to impair national security," which could lead to tariffs or restrictions on the sector.

Also on the economic side, industrial production dipped slightly more than expected in March to 0.3% M/M, weaker than the -0.2% consensus figure.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also expected to speak later in the day.

data.title: Valero set to shut Benicia refinery in California, books $1.1B charge
data.description:
Valero Energy (NYSE:VLO) said Wednesday it has submitted notice to the California Energy Commission that it plans to idle, restructure, or cease refining operations at its Benicia refinery by April 2026, while it evaluates strategic alternatives for its remaining operations in California.

Valero (NYSE:VLO) said it recorded a combined $1.1B pre-tax impairment charge for the Benicia and Wilmington refineries in the state, which it expects to be treated as a special item and excluded from Q1 adjusted earnings; the amount also included the recognition of expected asset retirement obligations of $337M as of March 31.

California regulators slapped Valero (VLO) with a record-breaking $82M fine last year for failing to report excess toxic emissions from the Benicia refinery that occurred during 2003-19 and were not disclosed to the public until 2022.

The Benicia city council has planned to vote on a controversial industrial safety ordinance which would create a citizens oversight commission, boost community air quality monitoring and empower city officials to issue fines for safety and air quality violations; Valero (VLO) has threatened to sue the city if it moves ahead with the ordinance.

data.title: Lyft gains with the FreeNow acquisition seen opening up market share opportunities in Europe
data.description:
Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) pushed higher in early trading on Wednesday after the company announced the acquisition of FreeNow earlier in the day.

FreeNow is a leading European mobility service provider headquartered in Hamburg, Germany. Founded in 2019 as a joint venture between BMW Group (OTC:BMWKY) and Mercedes-Benz Mobility (OTCPK:MBGAF), FreeNow operates as a multi-mobility app with a strong focus on taxi services, but also offers a wide range of urban transport options, including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, eScooters, eBikes, and eMopeds. FreeNow operates in over 150 cities across Ireland, the UK, Germany, Greece, Spain, Italy, Poland, France and Austria.

Piper Sandler highlighted that the FreeNow acquisition should roughly double Lyft's (NASDAQ:LYFT) addressable market. Analyst Thomas Champion said the $197 million price tag for the European growth opportunity feels reasonable. He noted that around 50% of taxi bookings in Europe still occur offline, which gives the company a significant market share.

Separately, Oppenheimer started off coverage on Lyft (LYFT) on Wednesday with an Outperform rating. The firm expects the rideshare industry will continue to challenge the rising cost of car ownership, sustaining double-digit growth for multiple marketplaces. Robotaxi technology's ability to lower fares over time is seen representing further upside. Crucially, analyst Chad Larkin and his team think that Lyft (LYFT) shares are already factoring in the tariff impact at their current levels.

Shares of Lyft (LYFT) were up 1.1% in mid-morning trading.

data.title: Former SEC Chair Gary Gensler sounds alarm on Trump’s tariff strategy
data.description:
Former SEC Chair Gary Gensler warned that President Donald Trump’s aggressive tariff strategy risks destabilizing U.S. markets and weakening diplomatic leverage with China, according to remarks made during a recent CNBC interview.

Gensler noted policy uncertainty has already caused “choppy” financial conditions due to policy uncertainty.

During the interview, he emphasized the importance of consistent diplomacy, recalling his bipartisan negotiations that secured a 50-page compliance agreement with Chinese regulators.

“We treated China with respect but with firmness,” he said, adding that Beijing had largely honored the deal during his tenure.

He also cautioned that abrupt policy shifts undermine credibility, noting Chinese officials “lean back and don’t engage” when facing inconsistency.

The ex-regulator criticized the administration’s “all of the above” trade approach as counterproductive, comparing it to a “self-inflicted injury” for markets and alliances. He told interviewers that China perceives time as its ally, observing “they think they can outweigh the volatility of the policy on the other side” during erratic negotiations.

In addition, he warned the strategy could exacerbate instability in Treasury markets and corporate investment plans.

On insider trading concerns, Gensler stressed the need for stricter ethics rules after reports of lawmakers trading around tariff announcements.

“It’s really critical that folks don’t trade on inside information,” he said, praising the SEC’s internal ban on individual stock trades as a model. He urged Congress to adopt similar restrictions to maintain public trust.

Gensler concluded that market turbulence and business hesitation will persist without coherent trade policies.

“Businesses are pulling back – they have to be much more careful about investments,” he noted, linking the uncertainty to weakened economic confidence.
